title: Interactive fiction story graph
description: Machine-read source of truth for a branching (choose-your-path) story, at projects/{slug}/if/{lang}/{slug}.if.json. The prose a reader sees is rendered from this graph into a sibling .md (anchored sections + in-page choice links). The deterministic graph gate (scripts/check-storygraph.mjs) enforces structural integrity: unique node ids, a real start node, every choice target resolves, every node is reachable, no dead-ends that are not endings, and a reachable ending. This is the interactive-fiction analogue of the novel state file — structured narrative the gate can verify.

start.description: Node id the reader begins at; must exist in nodes.

nodes.description: Every scene in the story. node.id is unique; choices reference other node ids.

id.description: Stable slug id; referenced by start + choice.target.

title.description: Human-readable scene heading for the rendered .md (e.g. 'The empty station'). Optional; the renderer falls back to the id.

text.description: The scene prose the reader sees at this node.

ending.description: True => a terminal node. A node with no choices is treated as an ending too.

choices.description: The branches out of this node. Omit (or empty) for an ending.
